content: Non-pharmaceutical interventions (NPIs) are public health measures that don't require medications or vaccines to reduce disease transmission. Categories include: personal protective measures (masks, hand hygiene, respiratory etiquette), environmental measures (ventilation, surface cleaning), and social distancing measures (physical distancing, school closures, workplace modifications, gathering limits, stay-at-home orders). NPIs work by reducing contact rate, transmission probability per contact, or infectious period. Evidence quality varies: masks and ventilation have strong mechanistic and observational support; school closures show mixed effectiveness depending on community transmission levels.
source: WHO NPI guidelines, CDC pandemic preparedness documents
